title: Restore Deleted Files
excerpt: We’d love the ability to restore a deleted file from the files column. This is not a feature at this time and is a pain-point for our team. Is there a way deleted files could just go to the trash like everything else?

posts.author: Hannah_Albert (Hannah Albert)
posts.cookedHtml: We’d love the ability to restore a deleted file from the files column. This is not a feature at this time and is a pain-point for our team. Is there a way deleted files could just go to the trash like everything else?

posts.author: KunjRajput (KunjRajput)
posts.cookedHtml:
To manually recover deleted files, check the Recycle Bin first. If it’s not there, try using Windows’ “Previous Versions” feature, which restores files from system backups. If no backups are available, the manual recovery might be difficult.

For an efficient solution, the BLR Data Recovery Tool or other program can help restore deleted files quickly and easily, even if they bypassed the Recycle Bin. It scans your drive for recoverable data, allowing you to recover your files with just a few clicks.

posts.author: Gary_ProBackup (Gary_ProBackup)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Hannah,

This might be a bit late, but I hope it’s still helpful. I completely understand how frustrating it is to lose files and not have an easy way to get them back, it’s definitely a pain point for many teams.

To help with this, our team built a backup solution that runs daily and keeps a secure copy of your data, including files. It also makes restoring deleted items much easier, which might save your team a lot of time and effort.

If you’d like to learn more, you can check out ProBackup for Monday.com.

Disclaimer: I’m a Customer Success Manager at ProBackup.

posts.author: sara.johnston (Sara Johnston)
posts.cookedHtml: It is absolutely critical that we have the ability to restore deleted files! My team has been using the Files Column more and more because we love the version history functionality and the visibility. Files we attach here are usually more important than files we attach in updates or directly in the Files library. It is totally unacceptable for these files to not follow the same deletion procedure as all the others.
